workLog.task: Issue #2205: Copilot CLI preToolUse/sessionStart hooks failed on Windows with 'can't open file C:\Users\user\hooks\sessionStart\...py: No such file or directory'. The generated hooks.json invoked scripts via python3 -u "./hooks/<event>/<script>.py" with cwd ".", which Copilot CLI resolves against the user's working directory, not the vendored plugin install location.
workLog.outcome: Changed build/scripts/generate_hooks.py:_build_copilot_entry to anchor script paths to the plugin install root using Copilot CLI's plugin-root environment variable. bash uses ${COPILOT_PLUGIN_ROOT:-${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}} (parameter-expansion fallback to the Claude-compatible alias), powershell uses $env:COPILOT_PLUGIN_ROOT. Regenerated src/copilot-cli/hooks/hooks.json (command strings only; no reordering or script-body changes). Added regression test test_generator_anchors_script_path_to_plugin_root.
workLog.evidence: Real-world Copilot CLI plugins (prp-core, dx-core, nowledge-mem, worktale) anchor hook commands with ${COPILOT_PLUGIN_ROOT}; GitHub docs confirm Copilot exposes ${COPILOT_PLUGIN_DATA} with ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} alias. pytest tests/build_scripts/test_generate_hooks.py -> 91 passed.

nextSteps: Bump src/copilot-cli/.claude-plugin/plugin.json 0.5.6 -> 0.5.7 so installs re-sync the corrected hooks.json (pre-push plugin-version-bump guard)
Empirically verify on a Windows Copilot CLI install that $env:COPILOT_PLUGIN_ROOT is populated for hook processes
